question_text: A coal power station burns 1000 tonnes of coal per day to generate electricity. A wind farm produces the same amount of electricity using wind turbines. Which statement best explains why the wind farm is considered more sustainable?

options.label: A
options.text: Wind turbines produce electricity without using up a natural resource

options.label: B
options.text: Wind turbines are cheaper to build than coal power stations

options.label: C
options.text: Wind turbines produce more electricity per day than coal power stations

options.label: D
options.text: Wind turbines do not require any maintenance during their lifetime

correct_answer: Wind turbines produce electricity without using up a natural resource
explanation: Wind is a renewable energy resource that is naturally replenished continuously, so it never runs out. Coal is a non-renewable resource that gets used up and cannot be replaced. This makes wind energy more sustainable because it does not deplete finite natural resources like fossil fuels do.
